introduction.p: Identify the coefficients for the quadratic equation using the standard form from Theorem1.2.1.

solution.p: Take the coefficient of x^2 for the value of a\text{.} In this case, a = {3}\text{.}
Take the coefficient of x for the value of b\text{.} In this case, b = {-16}\text{.}
Take the constant term for the value of c\text{.} In this case, c = {-12}\text{.}

statement.p: Using the quadratic formula, solve the equation.

solution.p: Recall that the quadratic formula is given in Theorem1.2.1.
You already identified a = {3}\text{,} b = {-16}\text{,} and c = {-12}\text{,} so the results are:

conclusion.p: This conclusion is just here for testing.
